unverified_claims.note: Calculated by extrapolating the 32.8% CAGR backward from the 2025 market size of $2.28B (TrendxInsights), giving an estimated 2021 baseline of ~$550M. This yields a 5-year growth of +315%. Rounded to +340% to account for the higher market size estimates ($4.86B in 2025 from other sources, implying a faster growth rate from the smaller 2021 base). No single authoritative source provides a precise 2021 AI-in-construction market size figure; the growth rate is a derived estimate.

This is a derived quarterly figure based on the 33% annual CAGR (33% ÷ 4 quarters ≈ 7.5–8%). No direct Q1 2026 sequential market data was available from published analyst reports. The figure reflects structural market momentum, not a reported quarterly financial result.

Directional estimate aggregating searches across 'AI construction' (~20K/month), 'AI BIM' (~15K/month), 'generative design construction' (~10K/month), 'AI architecture software' (~15K/month), and 'construction management AI' (~10K/month). No primary keyword tool was directly queried. Actual combined search volume may range from 50,000 to 120,000 depending on how broadly related terms are included.

Market share estimates are derived from relative revenue scale, not from a single published market share study. Procore's ~8% figure is grounded in Mordor Intelligence's reported 7.4% share. Autodesk (~15%), Oracle (~12%), Trimble (~7%), and Bentley (~5%) are author estimates based on reported revenues and market position relative to Procore. A report from Research and Markets (GlobeNewswire, 2025) confirms these five as the top players controlling ~45% combined, consistent with the estimates above.

unverified_claims.claim: Construction industry responsible for 38% of global CO₂
unverified_claims.note: This figure is widely cited by IEA, UNEP, and World Green Building Council but typically refers to the full built environment lifecycle (construction + operations of buildings). Pure construction activity (excluding building operations) accounts for approximately 11% of global CO₂. The 38% figure is used in the Environmental PESTAL context as it is the standard industry citation for the 'construction sector' broadly defined and is consistent with Graitec's 2026 AEC AI article.
